title: Trophis zeylanica
intro: Trophis zeylanica, also known as the Ceylon climbing fig or Indian climbing fig, is a climbing plant species native to Sri Lanka and other parts of Asia, characterized by its woody stems and large, dark green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Trophis zeylanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine and folk remedies, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
taxonomy: Trophis zeylanica belongs to the family Moraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the mulberry family. This family consists of around 1,000 species, including figs, mulberries, and banyans. The genus Trophis, to which T. zeylanica belongs, is native to tropical Asia and is characterized by its woody vines or shrubs. Trophis zeylanica is a species of evergreen vine native to Sri Lanka, known for its unique, heart-shaped leaves. The plant is often used in traditional medicine and as an ornamental plant in tropical regions.
uses: Trophis zeylanica has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its bark and leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and skin problems. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat wounds and reduce inflammation. Its medicinal properties make it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
benefits: Trophis zeylanica, also known as the Indian horse shoe tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and protect against cell damage. Its extracts have been studied for their potential to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant is thought to possess analgesic and antipyretic properties, which may help to alleviate pain and reduce fever. properties: Trophis zeylanica is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine in Sri Lanka. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves are also valued for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. constituents: Trophis zeylanica, also known as the Indian cork tree or Ceylon fig, contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and tannins. These compounds are believed to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Studies have identified specific compounds such as scopoletin and scopoletin glucoside, which are known for their biological activities. parts: Trophis zeylanica, commonly known as the Indian Cork Tree, is a plant with various medicinal properties. preparations: Trophis zeylanica, commonly known as the Indian fig or Ceylonese fig, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. side_effects: Trophis zeylanica, a plant native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa, is known for its medicinal properties and uses.
